steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The "but" announces a reversal: while Greek/Roman sculpture is sometimes characterized as idealizing, the passage reveals that many sculptures actually depict people in a different way. The blank must express the opposite of idealization.

steps.title: Match the contrasting concept
steps.content:
The passage says sculpture is "sometimes characterized as idealizing" but then contrasts that claim. The blank needs a word meaning the opposite of idealizing.

A. Unflattering means showing someone in a bad or unappealing light—the direct opposite of idealizing. "Depict people in decidedly unflattering ways" directly contradicts the idealized image. Correct.

B. Superficial means shallow or lacking depth. Superficial depictions aren't the opposite of idealized ones—a sculpture can be both shallow and flattering. Eliminated.

C. Imprecise means not exact or accurate. Imprecision says nothing about whether subjects are shown favorably or unfavorably. Eliminated.

D. Implausible means unconvincing or hard to believe. An implausible depiction doesn't contrast with an idealized one in the way the passage requires. Eliminated.

A is correct.
